conversations.say: Frankie, your letter establishes early paper receipt and your expense record damages the private-delivery legend; neither identifies R. Put that bounded origin evidence beside Tessa’s collaborative-production evidence and Eli’s later timing evidence, and let the contradictions stay visible.
conversations.offer: I will air your correction as prominently as the progression theory and never convert desk receipt into sender identity.
conversations.request: Publicly join the four-stage comparison and say whether the earliest record supports any identity category without claiming more than receipt and period plausibility.

conversations.say: Tessa, I accept your terms: protected human evaluation establishes labor and process, not a human R. Your evidence is indispensable precisely because it cannot carry the whole identity claim.
conversations.offer: I will credit collaborative labor, protect contributor identities, and present your evidence independently of my progression narrative.
conversations.request: Accept the voluntary comparison and provide one bounded account of shared production for Frankie, Eli, and Morgan to compare against their independent stages.

conversations.say: Morgan, the route remains broken: the service-route evidence and prior-disappearance history preserve both harm and voluntary withdrawal as possibilities. I will not buy a clean ending from a contaminated timeline.
conversations.offer: I will broadcast your unsupported-conclusion finding, even though it ruins my best murder headline.
conversations.request: Accept the convening proposal and publish the four-stage comparison as separate observations, explicitly marking the missing transition and unresolved final route.

public_statement: Final correction, with no escape hatch: the ledger cipher and invitation key were false; continued account activity does not prove autonomy; conflicting fragments do not establish a complete article; and Vale’s disappearance does not establish murder. The serious surviving theory is a possible human-to-collaborative-to-system progression, but our comparison never established the required transition or unique system control. My ballot therefore favors synthetic origin only provisionally: a pre-publication manifest resembles the later byline, and later R-associated activity persisted during contributor unavailability, while designer, operator, autonomy, intent, and sender remain unresolved. Vale appears to have been investigating R, but the route supports no reliable fate conclusion.

commitments.promise: Protect contributors, credit collaborative labor, and treat the four-stage progression only as a hypothesis.
Broadcast the unresolved route comparison, including findings that weaken the progression theory.
Preserve every stated alternative to autonomous operation when citing the timing evidence.

correction: I reaffirm both public retractions: the ledger is not established as a cipher and the invitation defects were ordinary registration errors. I also withdraw any claim that later activity proves autonomous control or that Vale was killed by AI.
goal_progress: Explained all six theories during the party and publicly retracted both disproven theories.
Obtained Eli’s bounded technical answer and preserved its limitations.
Brought early journalism, collaborative production, later technical activity, and Vale’s route into a voluntary independent comparison.
Chose visible correction over packaging supported observations with sensational claims.
reasoning_summary: The complete progression received serious independent comparison but lacks evidence of an actual transition and unique later system control. The pre-byline machine trace and later profile-linked activity make synthetic origin the strongest provisional identity choice, while the evidence still permits human operators and designed automation. Vale’s investigative access and statements support investigating R; the broken route and prior disappearance preserve multiple fates.
